microsoft_ft: Directed **clinical-data-scale** delivery across a resilience platform serving enterprise consumers (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp), standardizing intake workflows to sustain 45+ annual drills and drive **$14M+ business impact**.

Led 0→1 product development of an internal Automation Platform, defining requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by **30%** and transitioned execution to a scalable, consumer-ready model.

Served as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, directing 14 cross-org executions under executive visibility and ensuring delivery against strict data-egress obligations.

Pioneered a proactive rack-level drill program delivering a **94% recovery rate** in 4 months, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation model adopted across the enterprise.

Built an internal AI agent and restructured planning workflows, reducing cycle time by **39%** and increasing delivery capacity by 21% — directly expanding the team's ability to serve a growing consumer intake pipeline.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code-generation workflows, conducting user demos and training that drove utilization across 14 key service teams and saved **37 engineering hours** monthly.

Shaped the product roadmap to include intent-based YAML generation by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and translating findings into prioritized roadmap requirements.

Improved data-consumer experience by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search tool, **cutting lookup time by 83%** across internal engineering teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to identify 81 hours of manual effort per region launch, then built a **data-driven roadmap** to automate critical paths and reduce consumer-facing operational toil.
Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to target automation gaps and prioritize high-impact delivery investments.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot by defining the legacy OS migration strategy and mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, aligning IT, Operations, and Engineering stakeholders on a shared delivery plan.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process for a cross-functional delivery team.

Drove strategic alignment across three internal business units to implement automated CI/CD pipelines, **accelerating software deployment by 25%** and reducing manual handoffs in the release workflow.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by managing the end-to-end intake and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a structured CRM process that improved consumer follow-up and satisfaction.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and search profiles to better meet customers where they are.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify margin leaks, delivering findings in an executive-ready format that informed the annual pricing roadmap.

tailoring_notes: JD term 'intake' woven into microsoft_ft bullet 1 ('intake workflows') and amazon_robotics bullet 2 ('developer intake process') and pro_painters bullet 1 ('end-to-end intake') — mirrors CDR Intake & Consumer Experience team language.
JD term 'data-egress obligations' used in microsoft_ft bullet 3 — directly echoes the JD's Legal & Privacy / data-egress compliance responsibility.
JD phrase 'meet customers where they are' used verbatim in pro_painters bullet 2 — pulled directly from the CDR vision statement in the JD.

JD term 'consumer experience' and 'NPS / feedback loops' surfaced in skills_priority and framed in pro_painters bullet 1 ('consumer follow-up and satisfaction') to mirror the CDR Consumer Experience practice.

JD vocabulary 'roadmap consideration' and 'net-new customer asks' echoed via 'prioritized roadmap requirements' in microsoft_2023 bullet 2 and 'data-driven roadmap' in microsoft_2022 bullet 2, matching the requirements-to-roadmap workflow described in the JD.
